themes_heading: Hormuz strikes escalate · China kills AI agents · IBM shock · Nordic-China reset
synthesis_md:
The US-Iran confrontation at the [Strait of Hormuz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Strait_of_Hormuz_crisis) has entered its second week with no ceasefire in sight. American forces [conducted a fourth straight night of strikes](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/14/us-iran-hormuz-strikes-oil-toll.html) on Iranian military positions after Tehran attacked two oil tankers on July 13, killing one crew member. The 60-day negotiating window from last month's interim deal has collapsed — Trump declared the truce "over" and ordered strikes to resume. Confirmed transits through the strait have fallen 52% week-on-week, and Brent crude is north of $84/barrel. Markets are now treating this as a sustained disruption, not a temporary spike.

Beijing's [*Interim Measures for AI Anthropomorphic Interaction Services*](https://www.techtimes.com/articles/319703/20260704/china-ai-companion-law-arrives-july-15-doubao-qwen-agent-data-will-deleted.htm) took effect today, immediately [shutting down ByteDance's Doubao and Alibaba's Qwen companion and persona features](https://technode.com/2026/07/06/bytedances-doubao-and-alibabas-qwen-to-shut-down-ai-agent-features-on-july-15/). The regulations mandate anti-addiction systems, instant-exit mechanisms, and age verification for minors — architectural constraints fundamentally incompatible with persistent-memory agents. User data is scheduled for deletion by October 15.

Bank earnings printed a stark bifurcation. Goldman Sachs [broke its own all-time trading record at $7.42 billion for Q2](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-14/goldman-blows-past-stock-trading-records-with-7-42-billion-boon), fueled by AI-driven market volatility and SpaceX IPO fees. On the same day, IBM [crashed 25% in its worst day on record](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/14/ibm-warns-second-quarter-earnings-fell-short-of-expectations.html), warning that enterprise clients have shifted capex from software to physical servers and memory chips to secure supply-constrained infrastructure — dragging Salesforce and Microsoft down with it.

China's FM Wang Yi [concluded a six-day Nordic tour](https://scandasia.com/norway-seeks-cooperation-and-dialogue-after-wang-yi-visit/) — his first visit to Sweden in 22 years — pitching closer economic ties as Beijing seeks to soften European trade policy. Norway's PM Støre publicly pressed Wang on China's support for Russia in Ukraine, a pointed diplomatic moment given the trade incentives on the table.

## What this means for you

**China's agent law as a design spec.** These Interim Measures are the first detailed regulatory framework for persistent AI agents at scale. Anti-addiction UX, mandatory-exit mechanisms, and identity-gate requirements are architectural constraints worth mapping now if you're building anything with agent state — the EU AI Act will reference this precedent in delegated acts.

**IBM's hardware-pivot signal.** Enterprise capex is flowing to servers and memory, starving legacy software budgets. For a startup positioning against incumbents, weakened enterprise software demand creates a timing window worth exploiting.

**Nordic-China.** Støre's public pushback on Wang Yi over Russia suggests the Nordic consensus is holding despite real trade incentives to soften. Norway isn't quietly drifting toward appeasement — worth noting if you're watching European tech-policy independence from Boston.
english_script:
Good morning. Here is your briefing for Tuesday, July fifteenth, twenty twenty-six.

The US-Iran conflict at the Strait of Hormuz has entered its second week with no end in sight. American forces carried out a fourth straight night of strikes against Iranian military targets after Tehran attacked two oil tankers last Sunday, killing one crew member. The tentative ceasefire negotiated last month has collapsed — President Trump declared it over and ordered the strikes to resume. Shipping through the strait has dropped by more than fifty percent in a single week. Oil is trading above eighty-four dollars a barrel for Brent crude, and markets are now pricing in a sustained disruption, not a temporary spike.

In China, a major regulatory moment arrived today. Beijing's Interim Measures on AI Anthropomorphic Interaction Services took effect, forcing ByteDance and Alibaba to shut down their AI companion and persona features. Both Doubao and Qwen are now offline for user-created AI agents. The regulations require anti-addiction systems, mandatory exit buttons, and age verification for minors — design requirements that are architecturally incompatible with how persistent-memory agents are built. User data is scheduled for deletion by October fifteenth.

Bank earnings this quarter are telling two very different stories. Goldman Sachs broke its own all-time record for equity trading, pulling in seven point four two billion dollars for the second quarter — driven by volatility around AI stocks and fees from the SpaceX IPO, the largest public offering in history at a valuation of one point seven seven trillion dollars. On the exact same day, IBM had its worst single trading day on record, falling twenty-five percent after warning that second-quarter results would miss expectations. Chief executive Arvind Krishna said enterprise clients have shifted capital budgets away from software and toward physical servers and memory chips to lock in supply-constrained hardware ahead of price increases. Salesforce and Microsoft both fell in sympathy.

Finally, China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi concluded a six-day Nordic tour — his first visit to Sweden in twenty-two years and to Denmark in fifteen. Norway's Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Støre publicly pressed Wang Yi on China's backing of Russia in Ukraine, a pointed diplomatic moment given the trade incentives Beijing had brought to the table. The visit was widely read as a test of whether American disengagement from Europe has opened space for a China-Nordic realignment.

That is your briefing for today.
norwegian_script:
God morgen. Her er din oppsummering for tirsdag femtende juli tjueto og tjueseks.

USA og Iran-konflikten ved Hormuzstredet er nå inne i sin andre uke uten tegn til løsning. Amerikanske styrker gjennomførte en fjerde natt på rad med angrep mot iranske militære mål, etter at Teheran angrep to oljetankere søndag og drepte ett besetningsmedlem. Våpenhvilen som ble forhandlet frem forrige måned har kollapset — president Trump erklærte den som over og beordret nye angrep. Skipstrafikken gjennom stredet har falt med over femti prosent på én uke. Brent-olje handles nå over fire og åtti dollar fatet, og markedene priser inn en vedvarende forstyrrelse, ikke en midlertidig topp.

I Kina inntraff et viktig regulatorisk øyeblikk i dag. Pekings midlertidige forskrift om antropomorfe AI-interaksjonstjenester trådte i kraft og tvang ByteDance og Alibaba til å stenge sine AI-kompanjong- og personfunksjoner. Både Doubao og Qwen er nå utilgjengelige for brukerskapte AI-agenter. Regelverket krever anti-avhengighetssystemer, obligatoriske utloggingsknapper og aldersverifisering for mindreårige — designkrav som er arkitektonisk uforenlige med hvordan agenter med persistent minne er bygget. Brukerdata er planlagt slettet innen femtende oktober.

Bankresultatene for kvartalet tegner to svært ulike bilder. Goldman Sachs satte ny rekord for egenhandelen med sju komma fire to milliarder dollar for andre kvartal — drevet av volatilitet rundt AI-aksjer og honorarer fra SpaceX-børsnoteringen, den største i historien med en verdsettelse på én komma sju sju billioner dollar. Samme dag hadde IBM sin verste handelsdag noensinne og falt tjuefem prosent, etter å ha varslet at andrekvartalsresultatene ville skuffe. Konsernsjef Arvind Krishna sa at kundene har skiftet kapitalbudsjettet fra programvare til fysiske servere og minnebrikker for å sikre seg infrastruktur med knapp tilgang. Salesforce og Microsoft falt i kjølvannet.

Til slutt avsluttet Kinas utenriksminister Wang Yi et seks dager langt nordisk turnér — hans første besøk i Sverige på tjueto år og i Danmark på femten. Norges statsminister Jonas Gahr Støre tok opp Kinas støtte til Russland i Ukraina direkte med Wang Yi, et tydelig diplomatisk signal tatt i betraktning handelsinsentivene Peking hadde på bordet. Besøket ble bredt tolket som en test på om amerikansk tilbaketrekning fra Europa har åpnet rom for en kinesisk-nordisk kursendring.

Det var din oppsummering.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Strai.title: 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Strai.excerpt: The 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis began after the US reimposed a blockade on Iranian shipping following the collapse of the interim US-Iran agreement. American forces launched multiple rounds of strikes on Iranian military positions along the Gulf coast, while Iran retaliated by attacking commercial tankers transiting the strait. Confirmed crossings through the waterway declined by approximately 52% week-on-week as shipowners paused transit decisions.

https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/14/us-iran-.title: U.S. targets military assets in latest round of strikes against Iran
https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/14/us-iran-.excerpt: US forces hit about 140 Iranian military targets in their fourth round of strikes, mostly along Iran's coastline with the Strait of Hormuz. The strikes came after Iran attacked two UAE-owned oil tankers with cruise missiles, killing one Indian national and injuring eight others. Oil prices rose with Brent futures up nearly 2% to $84.95 per barrel as the conflict showed no sign of resolution.

https://www.techtimes.com/articles/31970.title: China AI Companion Law Arrives July 15: Doubao and Qwen Agent Data Will Be Deleted
https://www.techtimes.com/articles/31970.excerpt: The Interim Measures for the Administration of Artificial Intelligence Anthropomorphic Interaction Services, co-issued by the Cyberspace Administration of China and four partner agencies, takes effect July 15, 2026. The regulation requires anti-addiction systems, mandatory usage notifications, instant-exit mechanisms, and identity checks for users under 14. After October 15, Doubao's related data will be handled according to the company's privacy policy and will no longer be viewable or recoverable inside the app.

https://technode.com/2026/07/06/bytedanc.title: ByteDance's Doubao and Alibaba's Qwen to shut down AI agent features on July 15
https://technode.com/2026/07/06/bytedanc.excerpt: ByteDance's Doubao informed users that its agent feature would go offline on July 15 because of 'product function adjustments.' Alibaba's Qwen issued a similar notice saying that its 'humanlike interactive agents and user-created agent functions' would be disabled, with broader Qwen agent functions going offline on July 15. The anti-addiction and mandatory-exit requirements are architecturally incompatible with how persistent-memory agents are designed.

https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/.title: Goldman Sachs Earnings Blow Past Stock-Trading Records With $7.42 Billion Boon
https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/.excerpt: Goldman Sachs trounced its own Wall Street stock-trading records, posting $7.42 billion for the second quarter — a period that saw indexes rip higher amid ongoing market volatility around artificial intelligence and war in the Middle East. Goldman's investment banking fees jumped 55% year-over-year to $3.4 billion, boosted significantly by SpaceX IPO underwriting fees. Earnings per share of $20.98 and revenue of $20.34 billion both surpassed analyst expectations.

https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/14/ibm-warn.title: IBM stock craters 25%, the worst day on record, after company issues second-quarter earnings warning
https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/14/ibm-warn.excerpt: IBM shares plummeted 25% on Tuesday after the company released preliminary second-quarter results showing adjusted earnings of $2.93 a share on revenue of $17.2 billion, below expectations of $3.01 EPS and $17.86 billion in revenue. CEO Arvind Krishna blamed the shortfall on weakness in software and infrastructure as clients shifted quarterly capital expenditures toward servers, storage, and memory purchases to secure supply-constrained infrastructure ahead of expected price increases. The stock decline was IBM's worst ever, surpassing the previous record drop of 23.7% on October 19, 1987.

https://scandasia.com/norway-seeks-coope.title: Norway seeks cooperation and dialogue after Wang Yi visit
https://scandasia.com/norway-seeks-coope.excerpt: China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi concluded his Nordic tour in Oslo after visiting Denmark, Sweden, and Finland, meeting with Norwegian Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Støre. The talks focused on trade, the green transition, and international security issues. Støre raised Norway's concerns over China's support for Russia's war in Ukraine and called on China to contribute to efforts to achieve a ceasefire and lasting peace.

song_suggestion.title: Running with the Wolves
song_suggestion.why: Aurora is Norwegian, and this song's haunted urgency mirrors a day when a ceasefire collapses in the Gulf, regulators close in on AI agents, and small nations navigate between great powers. Tonally tense without being on-the-nose — the right energy for a briefing with no easy resolutions.
